title: Round 2 — where does the recommendation live?
prompt: Pool update: seed-inference is killed for good (the sketch pad already covers rough input) and the structure dial is dropped. All five options here are syntheses of your four picks — gallery × concierge × meta-board × canvas-first. Common spine per your notes: brief-as-today drives a system-suggested method, methods stay named and explicit, mind map is one card among many, and choosing it opens the live co-edited canvas. What varies is WHERE the recommendation lives and HOW LOUD it is: a ribboned gallery (A), the first board itself as method chooser (B), one warm-up question (C), auto-routing with an inline change-gallery (D), or live prompt-seeded miniatures (E). Judge on time-to-first-spark vs sense of control — and note anything you'd steal between flows; the winner becomes the build-plan spine.

options.label: Recommended Gallery
options.description: Named gallery as today, but the best-fit card wears a recommended ribbon with its reason; picking Mind map opens the live canvas behind a slim change-anytime rail

options.label: Living Gallery
options.description: Every gallery card is a live miniature already seeded from the prompt; tapping the mind-map mini enlarges it and that same tree grows into the full canvas

survey.elaborationPrompt: Which flow (or combination) is the one we build? Anything to steal across flows — e.g. B's board grammar with A's ribbon, or E's live minis inside D's change menu?
